# Doge release-notes template

Fill the `<...>` placeholders, drop unused sections, keep the fixed parts (the
doge-speak tagline, `cargo install dogelang`, and the `compare/vPREV...vX.Y.Z`
link). The compare link only resolves once the `vX.Y.Z` tag is pushed.

```markdown
# What's New in vX.Y.Z

<doge-speak tagline, e.g. "Much X, very Y, wow.">

<one- or two-sentence summary of the release theme.>

## <Section — e.g. Language & types>

- **<Feature name>** (#<issue>) — <what it does, one to three sentences.>
- **<Feature name>** (#<issue>) — <what it does.>

## <Section — e.g. New stdlib modules>

- **`<module>` — <one-word purpose>** (#<issue>): <members and behaviour.>

## <Section — e.g. Improvements / Fixes>

- **<Change>** (#<issue>) — <what changed.>

## Install

`cargo install dogelang`


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/DogeLanguage/doge/compare/vPREV...vX.Y.Z
```
